stories.headline: What to Expect as Putin Arrives in China for Xi Talks
stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing to reinforce ties with Chinese leader Xi Jinping and make progress on a long-stalled energy project. Bloomberg's Stephen Engle breaks down what's at stake. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Senate advances resolution to curb Trump’s power to wage war on Iran
stories.summary: Vote to advance War Powers Resolution seen as rare rebuke of the president as pressure grows to end US attack on Iran.

stories.headline: Middle East energy crisis pushes India, Southeast Asia back toward coal
Trump’s ‘worst’ Republican critic loses most expensive House primary ever
stories.summary: Maverick Kentucky Congress member Thomas Massie defeated by challenger backed by president

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Japan CEO: Hope Yen to Gain to 140
stories.summary: Alberto Tamura, CEO at Morgan Stanley Japan, says he is hoping that the yen strengthens to around 140 against the dollar, and that action from the Bank of Japan is key for that to happen. He speaks with Shery Ahn from the sidelines of the Morgan Stanley and MUFG Japan Summit in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley's Garner on Japan, EM Markets
stories.summary: Jonathan Garner, Chief Asia and Emerging Market Equity Strategist at Morgan Stanley, discusses his outlook for the region's markets and investment strategy. He speaks with Shery Ahn from the sidelines of the Morgan Stanley and MUFG Japan Summit in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Here’s a Look at India’s Measures to Stem Hit From Oil Shock
stories.summary: India is rushing to contain the fallout of the Iran war, as soaring oil prices hammer the rupee and drive record foreign outflows from local stocks.

stories.headline: EU Expedites Start of US Trade Deal in Bow to Trump Demand
stories.summary: The European Union finalized the text of its long-delayed US trade deal after months of negotiations, clearing a major hurdle to ratifying the pact before President Donald Trump’s threatened deadline to impose higher tariffs.

stories.headline: EU approves trade deal with the US despite uncertainty in transatlantic relations
stories.summary: European negotiators agreed late on Tuesday to implement the controversial trade agreement concluded last summer with the US. However, the deal — signed in the Scottish city of Turnberry — remains fragile as long as US President Donald Trump continues to use tariffs as a tool of political pressure.

stories.headline: Oasis Mgmt Fischer on Investment Strategies
stories.summary:
Seth Fischer, Founder and CIO of Oasis Management, discusses his outlook and investment strategies for the Japanese market.
He speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud Watts from the sidelines of Sohn Hong Kong Investment Leaders Conference on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Beach Energy CEO on Business outlook
stories.summary:
Brett Woods, Managing Director and CEO of Beach Energy, discusses the outlook for the Australian oil and gas producer's business.
He speaks with Paul Allen on the sidelines of the Australian Energy Producers Conference & Exhibition in Adelaide on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Japan Head Says BOJ Hike Key to Strengthening Yen
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley’s Japan chief said he is hoping that the yen strengthens to around 140 against the dollar, and that Bank of Japan action is key for that to happen.

stories.headline: Singapore Tops Indonesia as Biggest Southeast Asian Stock Market
stories.summary: Indonesia continues to notch one grim milestone after another. The latest: losing its status as Southeast Asia’s largest stock market to Singapore.

stories.headline: Australia’s Beach Energy Sees M&A Potential on Domestic Gas Plan
stories.summary: Australia’s Beach Energy Ltd. believes it’s a more attractive acquisition target due to a government plan to force gas producers to keep a portion of their sales at home.

stories.headline: Jefferies Hires Volschenk to Lead Metals and Mining in EMEA
stories.summary: Jefferies Financial Group Inc. has hired Gideon Volschenk from Standard Chartered Plc to head its metals and mining banking team in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Bank Indonesia Rate Hike in Play as Rupiah Plumbs New Lows
stories.summary: Indonesia may see its first interest-rate hike in two years as the rupiah’s slide to record lows this week intensifies pressure for the central bank to mount a stronger defense of the currency.

stories.headline: Republican Primary for Georgia Governor Will Go to a Runoff
stories.summary: The state’s lieutenant governor, Burt Jones, will face a billionaire health care executive, Rick Jackson, on June 16 to decide the party’s standard-bearer to succeed Gov. Brian Kemp.

stories.headline: Thailand tightens visa rules for tourists, citing crime by foreigners
stories.summary: Move brings an end to a 60 day visa-free stay that was agreed with 93 countries, including the UK, US and much of EuropeThailand is drastically cutting the length of visa-free stays for tourists from more than 90 countries in an effort to curb crime involving foreign nationals, officials said on Tuesday.Tourism is vital to the south-east Asian nation’s economy, but foreign arrivals are yet to return to their pre-Covid levels. stories.headline: More than 15,800 people killed in Russia’s all-out war on Ukraine: UN
stories.summary: Nearly 800 children are among those killed in Ukraine since Russia's full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN says.

stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
stories.summary: Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

stories.headline: Estonia says Nato jet shot down drone over its territory
stories.summary: Estonia suspects the drone was a Ukrainian projectile knocked off course by Russian electronic jamming.

stories.headline: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
stories.summary: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.

stories.headline: Trump endorses Paxton in Texas Senate primary over incumbent Cornyn
stories.summary: The president faced pressure from his base to endorse the state’s attorney general, Ken Paxton. Advisers had urged him to support Sen. John Cornyn, arguing that Paxton has too much baggage.

stories.headline: Former governor general and Brisbane’s Anglican archbishop Peter Hollingworth dies at 91
stories.summary: Hollingworth, appointed governor general by John Howard in 2001, resigned over his handling of child sexual abuse in the Anglican churchFormer governor general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child sexual abuse in the Anglican church, has died.Hollingworth, who devoted much of his life to fighting poverty, served as the Anglican archbishop of Brisbane for 11 years from 1990, the first Australian-born person to hold the posit

stories.headline: Russian jamming blamed after Nato jet downs Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: Officials from Baltic states say Moscow behind latest such incident but also tell Kyiv to be more careful with its routingA Romanian F-16 Nato jet shot down a drone over Estonia on Tuesday in what appears to be the latest case of Russian electronic jamming diverting long-range Ukrainian drones into the alliance’s territory.A local resident told the Estonian public broadcaster, ERR, that he had seen two fighter jets – part of a Nato force policing

stories.headline: US authorities say missionary who contracted Ebola en route to Germany
stories.summary: World Health Organization says death toll from outbreak has risen to 134, as experts warn of continued challenges.

stories.headline: Thousands of protesters demand president’s resignation in Bolivia’s La Paz
stories.summary: Bolivia faces its worst economic crisis in 40 years, fueling nationwide protests demanding the president's resignation.

stories.headline: Trump backs Ken Paxton in crucial Texas Republican Senate run-off
stories.summary: Polls show mixed results, with Paxton and Cornyn in close battle against Democrat James Talarico.

stories.headline: UAE says mystery drones targeting nuclear plant came from Iraq
stories.summary: The UAE said drones that targeted its Barakah nuclear plant last week were launched from Iraq, indicating Tehran-backed militias have continued targeting Gulf energy infrastructure on behalf of Iran.

stories.headline: Israeli minister Smotrich says ICC prosecutor seeking warrant for his arrest

stories.headline: Israeli forces board Gaza-bound flotilla near Cyprus, activists say

stories.headline: Global Bond Selloff Threatens Turmoil in Weakest Asian Economies
stories.summary: Three of Asia’s most vulnerable economies are showing rising strains as their central banks come under pressure to tighten policy even as the economic hit from the Iran-war oil shock deepens.

stories.headline: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
stories.summary: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.

stories.headline: ‘Foolish’ CSIRO job cuts will mean Australia unable to provide climate projections to global reports, scientists warn
stories.summary: Exclusive: Science agency is planning to sack a third of the team working on the national climate model, sources sayFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastJob cuts at the national science agency mean Australia will no longer be able to submit climate projections to form part of global reports and will have significantly reduced ability to forecast future damage to the count

stories.headline: Tasmanian government apologises over stolen body parts scandal
stories.summary: An investigation found that 177 human specimens from dozens of bodies were secretly kept by a museum.

stories.headline: Trump told Taiwan not to 'go independent' - but does it want to?

stories.summary: Oil held steady as traders weighed President Donald Trump’s latest threat to resume strikes on Iran.

stories.headline: Threat Looms for Japan’s Stocks as Benchmark Bond Yield Nears 3%
stories.summary: Japanese stocks are under pressure as long-term interest rates approach 3%, levels which are stoking fears about a hit to corporate earnings via increased borrowing costs.

stories.headline: Toll Brothers Up As Luxury-Home Orders Hit Highest in Two Years
stories.summary: Toll Brothers shares rose as much as 5% in postmarket trading on Tuesday after the luxury homebuilder reported second-quarter profit that beat analysts’ estimates and raised its full-year guidance.

stories.headline: Investment Bank Lincoln International’s IPO Raises $421 Million
stories.summary: Mid-market investment bank Lincoln International Inc. and its backers raised nearly $421 million in an US initial public offering priced at the top of the marketed range.

stories.headline: Sen. Gillibrand of New York Clashes With Sean Duffy Over Funding Freeze
stories.summary: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, Democrat of New York, criticized the transportation secretary, Sean Duffy, for posting a video that bragged about withholding money from the state.

stories.headline: Minnesota Becomes First State to Ban Prediction Markets
stories.summary: Like at least other 14 states, Minnesota has wrestled with how to regulate the popular prediction market sites. Federal officials sued the state over its new law.

stories.summary: Zainab Sheriff unjustly sentenced to four years in prison for incitement and threatening language, say activistsLawyers, politicians and activists have called for the release of one of Sierra Leone’s best-known celebrities, who they said was unjustly imprisoned as part of a government crackdown on free speech and political dissent.Zainab Sheriff, a singer and reality-TV show contestant who became a political opposition figure, was sentenced in Ap

stories.headline: Belfast harbour operator to invest £1.3bn as NI economy grows
stories.summary: Port has upgraded offshore wind facilities and is to expand quays, ferry terminals and cruise ship servicesThe operator of Belfast harbour plans to spend £1.3bn over the next 25 years to take advantage of strong economic growth in Northern Ireland, in what would be one of the largest non-governmental investments in the region’s history.The Belfast Harbour Commissioners said the money would be spent on upgrading the port, with the possibility of r

stories.headline: ‘Each day counts’: EU scrambles to seal defence industry deal
stories.summary: European parliamentarians, EU member states and Commission mediators are in the thick of talks aimed at bolstering the bloc’s defence industry by cutting down on bureaucracy. Despite the political will for the continent to rearm itself, significant divisions have emerged on the details of delivery.

stories.headline: From raw materials to value-added exports: A structural trade transformation
stories.summary: Uzbekistan is broadening its export base through market diversification, industrial growth, and trade reforms, targeting higher value-added products and new international markets.

stories.headline: Brussels’ Manneken Pis goes sporty
stories.summary: Brussels’ iconic Manneken Pis statue received a makeover in honour of the annual World Fair Play Day. Celebrated on 19 May, the event shines a spotlight on  "fair play'' in the world of sport.

stories.headline: Trump administration unveils $1.8bn fund to compensate his allies as he drops tax lawsuit
stories.summary: The settlement will go towards individuals who allege they were subjected to unfair investigations.

stories.headline: Four killed in protests during Kenyan strikes over high fuel prices

stories.headline: Lebanon says six killed in Israeli strike as US announces ceasefire extension
stories.summary: President Trump announced the truce last month, but Israel and Hezbollah have continued to exchange fire since then.

stories.summary: The US president says Tehran has to show "real" commitment to removing nuclear fuel and stopping uranium enrichment.
